children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: In the administration of the programs established under sections 5621, 5622, and 5623(b) of this title the Secretary shall require by regulation each exporter or other participant under the program to maintain all records concerning a program transaction for a period of not to exceed 5 years after completion of the program transaction, and to permit the Secretary to have full and complete access, for such 5-year period, to such records.

children.heading: Confidentiality
children.label: 2 Confidentiality
children.text: The personally identifiable information contained in reports under subsection (a) may be withheld in accordance with . Any officer or employee of the Department of Agriculture who knowingly discloses confidential information as defined by shall be subject to . Nothing in this subsection shall be construed to authorize the withholding of information from Congress.section 552(b)(4) of title 5section 1905 of title 18section 1905 of title 18
hold such exporter, assignee, or participant liable for any and all losses to the Corporation resulting from such fraud or violation;
require a refund of any assistance provided to such exporter, assignee, or participant plus interest, as determined by the Secretary; and
collect liquidated damages from such exporter, assignee, or participant in an amount determined appropriate by the Secretary.

children.heading: Suspension and debarment
children.label: c Suspension and debarment
children.text: The Commodity Credit Corporation may suspend or debar for 1 or more years any exporter, assignee, or other participant from participation in one or more of the programs authorized by this chapter if the Corporation determines, after opportunity for a hearing, that such exporter, assignee, or other participant has violated the terms and conditions of the program or of this chapter and that the violation is of such a nature as to warrant suspension or debarment.

children.heading: False certifications
children.label: d False certifications
children.text: The provisions of shall apply to any false certifications issued under this chapter.section 1001 of title 18
